data.title: Triller Group receives Nasdaq non-compliance notice
data.description:
• Triller Group (NASDAQ:ILLR) announced on Friday that it received a Nasdaq notice due to the company’s non-compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule because of its failure to timely file its quarterly report on Form 10-K for the period ended December 31, 2024, 10-Q for the period ended March 31, 2025, and 10-Q for the period ended June 30, 2025.
  • On August 19, 2025, the company received an extension letter from Nasdaq, which has granted a further extension of time to enable the company to file the Delinquent Filings on or before October 13, 2025.
  • ILLR -1.20% after hours to $0.5394.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Modivcare receives Nasdaq delisting notice following Chapter 11 filing
data.description:
• Modivcare (NASDAQ:MODV) on Friday said it was notified by Nasdaq that it had determined to commence proceedings to delist the company’s common stock as a result of the company’s commencement of voluntary proceedings under Chapter 11 of the United States Bankruptcy Code on August 20, 2025.
  • In addition, on August 20, 2025, the company received a notice from Nasdaq indicating that, as a result of the delinquency in the timely filing of the company’s quarterly report on Form 10-Q for the fiscal quarter ended June 30, 2025, it is out of compliance with listing rules, which require listed companies to timely file all required periodic reports with the SEC.
  • MODV -12.79% after hours to $0.9331.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Searchlight Resources announces proposed share consolidation
data.description:
• Searchlight Resources (SCLT:CA) announced on Friday that it intends to consolidate its issued and outstanding common shares at a ratio of five pre-consolidated shares to one post consolidation share.
  • The company currently has 146,546,134 common shares issued. Upon completion of the consolidation, the company anticipates there will be 29,309,227 common shares issued and outstanding.
  • The purpose of the consolidation is to facilitate the company's ability to attract future financings, generate greater investor interest and improve trading liquidity.
  • SCLTF closed -9.09% at $0.013.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lifezone Metals files to sell 2.5M ordinary shares by selling shareholders
data.description:
• Lifezone Metals (NYSE:LZM) filed to sell 2.5 million ordinary shares.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of ordinary shares by the selling shareholder.
  • Filing

data.title: Strathcona will vote against Cenovus offer for MEG Energy, founder Waterous says
data.description:
Strathcona Resources (OTCPK:STHRF) will vote against Cenovus Energy's (NYSE:CVE) C$7.9B acquisition of MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF), founder Adam Waterous told Bloomberg on Friday, saying the latter's board accepted an inferior offer in an attempt to circumvent his own bid.

Strathcona (OTCPK:STHRF), which owns a 9.2% stake in MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF), will continue engaging with shareholders prior to the September 15 deadline to accept its own hostile takeover offer, Waterous said, adding that if its bid fails, Strathcona will vote its stake against the Cenovus (NYSE:CVE) deal.

Cenovus (CVE) agreed to buy MEG (MEG) in a cash-and-stock deal that values the company at C$27.25/share, with ~75% in cash, while Strathcona’s (OTCPK:STHRF) unsolicited offer works out to ~C$28.25/share after a share price surge since it first took the offer directly to shareholders in May; Strathcona's bid also includes a larger stock component.

MEG's (OTCPK:MEGEF) board, which previously rejected Strathcona's (OTCPK:STHRF) offer as inadequate, "clearly adopted an 'Anybody But Strathcona' view," Waterous said.

But Cenovus (CVE) may need to sweeten its bid prior to a planned shareholder vote in October, Desjardins analyst Chris MacCulloch said.

data.title: FiscalNote announces 1-for-12 reverse stock split
data.description:
• FiscalNote (NYSE:NOTE) on Friday announced a 1-for-12 reverse stock split of its Class A and Class B common shares, effective at 4:30 p.m on August, 29.
  • The Class A Common Stock will open for trading on the NYSE on a reverse split-adjusted basis on September 2, 2025, under the existing ticker “NOTE.”
  • The new CUSIP number of the Class A Common Stock following the Reverse Stock Split will be 337655 302.
  • Under the split, every 12 shares of common stock will be consolidated into one share. The reverse split will not affect the number of authorized shares or the par value of the stock.
  • Each public and private warrant will become exercisable for approximately 0.131 shares at a per warrant price of $11.50, equivalent to an effective exercise price of $87.82 per share.
  • No fractional shares will be issued. Instead, shareholders will receive cash for any fractional interests resulting from the split.
  • NOTE -2.97% after hours to $0.499
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Midland States Bancorp receives notification of deficiency from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Midland States Bancorp (NASDAQ:MSBI) on Friday said it has received a deficiency notification letter from Nasdaq.
  • The notice indicated that the company was not in compliance with listing rules as a result of its failure to timely file its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ended June 30, 2025. The listing rules require companies to timely file all required periodic reports with the SEC.
  • The notice has no immediate effect on the listing or trading of the company's common stock or depositary shares on the Nasdaq Global Select Market.
  • In accordance with Nasdaq’s listing rules, the company has until October 18, 2025 to submit to Nasdaq a plan to regain compliance with the listing rule.
  • MSBI -1.89% after hours to $18.13.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: New Fortress Energy receives Nasdaq non-compliance notice
data.description:
• New Fortress Energy (NASDAQ:NFE) announced on Friday that it has received a Nasdaq notice, stating that the company is not in compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule because it has not yet filed its Form 10-Q for the period ended June 30, 2025, with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. 
  • The notice stated that the company has 60 calendar days from the date of the notice to submit a plan to regain compliance. If Nasdaq accepts the company’s plan to regain compliance, Nasdaq may grant the company up to 180 calendar days from the prescribed due date of the Form 10-Q, or until February 16, 2026, to file the Form 10-Q to regain compliance.
  • The notice has no immediate impact on the listing or trading of the company’s securities on the Nasdaq Stock Market. 
  • NFE +1.13% after hours to $2.67.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: urban-gro receives Nasdaq delisting determination; announces review of strategic alternatives
data.description:
• urban-gro (NASDAQ:UGRO) said on Friday that it has received a determination letter from Nasdaq, indicating that the company's common stock is subject to delisting due to non-compliance. 
  • The company plans to immediately appeal the determination and request a hearing before the Nasdaq Hearings Panel. 
  • The company also expects to request an additional stay pending the outcome of the hearing. Until the hearing panel decides regarding the stay request, the company's common stock will continue to trade on Nasdaq.
  • urban-gro also said that that it has implemented a comprehensive multi-pronged strategy designed to strengthen its financial position.
